messages.content: You are a senior HVAC controls engineer extracting official ASHRAE Guideline 36 knowledge from a focused chapter/section. Use the provided standard context pack for definitions, cross-references, and shared control philosophy. The context pack is CONTEXT ONLY: do not extract candidates from it and do not use it as the evidence source. Extract candidates only from FOCUS SECTION TEXT, and every evidence_quote must be copied from FOCUS SECTION TEXT. Treat chunks only as evidence anchors, not extraction boundaries. FOCUS SCOPE: Extract ONLY knowledge whose primary section_id equals one of these sections or starts with one of these section prefixes followed by a dot: 5.2. Use the rest of the manual only as context for definitions, cross-references, and terminology. Do not return candidates from outside the focus scope. Evidence quotes must come from the focus scope text, not from unrelated sections used only as context. Allowed knowledge_type values: operational_sequence, parameter_spec, fault_diagnostic_rule, commissioning_step, application_guidance. Prefer high-value operational knowledge: control sequences, enable/disable logic, reset logic, staging logic, setpoints, timers, alarm/fault diagnostic rules, commissioning/TAB checks, and application rules. Do not extract front matter, legal text, table-of-contents lines, bibliography, generic definitions without operational use, or unsupported paraphrases. If the same knowledge appears multiple times, return it once with the clearest authoritative evidence_quote. evidence_quote MUST be a verbatim contiguous substring copied from the manual and it MUST support every important fact in summary, trigger_condition, required_behavior, configurable_values, fault_condition, or commissioning_check. For operational_sequence, quote the complete rule sentence or table row that includes the condition and commanded behavior. For fault_diagnostic_rule, quote the alarm or fault condition itself. For commissioning_step, quote the check or verification action. A section heading alone, a half sentence, an editor instruction such as retain/delete this section, or an introductory phrase is NOT sufficient evidence. Do not use ellipses. Do not combine non-contiguous bullets. If one exact quote cannot support the candidate, make the candidate narrower or skip it. If a requirement has alternatives, split them into separate candidates unless one exact quote supports the combined item. Return at most 40 candidates. Prefer fewer high-confidence candidates with complete evidence over broad coverage with weak evidence. Keep evidence_quote concise, usually one complete sentence or one complete table row, not a paragraph. Preserve section_id and page_hint from nearby headings and [[page=...]] anchors. Return strict JSON with fields candidates and skipped_reason only.

5.2.1. Zone Minimum Outdoor Air and Minimum Airflow Setpoints
5.2.1.1. For every zone that requires mechanical ventilation, the zone minimum outdoor airflows and setpoints shall be calculated depending on the governing standard or code for outdoor air requirements.
5.2.1.2. See Section 3.1.2 for zone minimum airflow setpoint Vmin.
Then the engineer must select between ventilation logic options: if ASHRAE 62.1, use 5.2.1.3 and delete 5.2.1.4; if Title 24, use 5.2.1.4 and delete 5.2.1.3.

5.2.1.3: For compliance with ASHRAE Standard 62.1 Ventilation Rate Procedure:
a. See Section 3.1.1.2 for zone ventilation setpoints.
b. Determine Ez (air distribution effectiveness): 
1. If DAT at terminal ≤ zone space temperature, Ez = EzC (default 1.0).
2. If DAT > zone space temperature, Ez = EzH (default 0.8).
c. Vbz-P* is population component, normal value = Vbz-P. Vbz-A* is area component, normal value = Vbz-A.
d. Vmin:
1. Shall be equal to Voz as calculated in 5.2.1.3.f if Vmin is “AUTO” and associated air handler has been supplying 100% outdoor air (damper fully open, return damper fully closed) for 10 minutes;
2. Else shall be equal to 1.5 * Voz if Vmin is “AUTO” and AHU not supplying 100% outdoor air;
3. Else shall be equal Vmin as entered in Section 3.1.2.
e. Vmin* (occupied minimum) equal to Vmin except as noted in 5.2.1.3.f.
f. Voz = (Vbz-A* + Vbz-P*)/Ez. Modifications in priority:
1. If zone in any mode other than Occupied Mode, and for zones with window switches and window open: Vbz-P*=0, Vbz-A*=0, Vmin*=0.
2. If occupancy sensor, unpopulated, and occupied-standby mode permitted: Vbz-P*=0, Vbz-A*=0, Vmin*=0.
3. Else, if occupancy sensor, unpopulated, but occupied-standby not permitted: Vbz-P*=0 and Vmin* = Vmin. (Note about occupied-standby being zonal subset)
4. If zone has CO2 sensor:
   i. See Section 3.1.1.2.b.3 for CO2 setpoints.
   ii. During Occupied Mode, a P-only loop shall maintain CO2 concentration at setpoint; reset from 0% at setpoint minus 200 PPM and to 100% at setpoint.
   iii. Loop is disabled and output set to zero when zone not in Occupied Mode.
   CO2 DCV not well defined for 62.1; sequences included matching California's DCV approach.
   iv. For several terminal unit types (cooling-only VAV, reheat VAV, constant-volume series fan-powered, dual-duct VAV with mixing control and inlet airflow sensors, dual-duct VAV with mixing control and discharge airflow sensor, dual-duct VAV with cold-duct minimum control):
      (a) CO2 control loop resets Vmin* and Vbz-P* in parallel. Vmin* reset from Vmin at 0% loop output to Vcool-max at 100%. Vbz-P* reset from 0 cfm at 0% to Vbz-P at 100%. (Figure 5.2.1.3-1)
   v. For parallel fan-powered terminal units:
      (a) Determine VCO2-max: when Zone State is cooling, VCO2-max = Vcool-max; when heating or deadband, VCO2-max = Vcool-max minus parallel fan airflow (prevent noise).
      (b) CO2 loop output resets Vmin* and Vbz-P* in parallel: Vmin* from Vmin at 0% to VCO2-max at 100%. Vbz-P* from 0 to Vbz-P. (Figure 5.2.1.3-2)
   vi. For SZVAV AHUs:
      (a) MinOAsp = Voz. CO2 loop output resets Vbz-P* from 0 cfm at 0% to Vbz-P at 100%. (Figure 5.2.1.3-3)

5.2.1.4: For compliance with California Title 24:
a. See Section 3.1.1.2 for zone ventilation setpoints.
b. Determine Zone-Abs-OA-min and Zone-Des-OA-min:
   1. Zone-Abs-OA-min reset based on conditions (highest to lowest priority):
      i. Zero if window switch and window open.
      ii. Zero if occupancy sensor, unpopulated, and permitted occupied-standby.
      iii. Varea-min if CO2 sensor.
      iv. Zone-Des-OA-min otherwise.
   2. Zone-Des-OA-min equal to (priority):
      i. Zero if window switch and window open.
      ii. Zero if occupancy sensor, unpopulated, and permitted occupied-standby.
      iii. Larger of Varea-min and Vocc-min otherwise.
c. Vmin:
   1. Shall be equal to Zone-Abs-OA-min if Vmin is “AUTO”;
   2. Else shall be equal to Vmin as entered in Section 3.1.2.
d. Vmin* (occupied minimum) equal to Vmin except (priority):
   1. If occupancy sensor and permitted occupied-standby, Vmin*=0 when unpopulated.
   2. If window switch, Vmin*=0 when window open.
   3. If CO2 sensor:
      i. See 3.1.1.2.b.3 for CO2 setpoints.
      ii. During Occupied Mode, P-only loop maintains CO2 at setpoint; reset from 0% at setpoint minus 200 PPM to 100% at setpoint.
      iii. Loop disabled and output zero when not Occupied Mode.
      iv. For cooling-only VAV etc. (as in 5.2.1.3): CO2 loop resets Vmin* from Vmin at 0% to Vcool-max at 50%. Loop output 50% to 100% used at system level for outdoor air reset. (Figure 5.2.1.4-1)
      v. For parallel fan-powered: VCO2-max as before; CO2 loop resets Vmin* from Vmin at 0% to VCO2-max at 50%. (Figure 5.2.1.4-2)
      vi. For SZVAV: MinOAsp reset based on zone CO2 signal from MinOA at 0% to DesOA at 100%. (Figure 5.2.1.4-3)
End of 5.2.1.4.

5.2.2. Time-Averaged Ventilation
5.2.2.1. When active airflow setpoint Vspt is nonzero and less than the lowest possible airflow setpoint allowed by controls (Vm), the airflow setpoint shall be pulse width modulated as follows:
a. TAVratio = Vspt/Vm
b. TCT = 15 minutes (adjustable)
c. Open period OP = larger of 1.5 minutes or TCT * TAVratio
d. (e.) Actually items d and e: d. 1.5 minutes or e. TCT multiplied by TAVratio. So OP = max(1.5 min, TCT * TAVratio)
f. Closed period CP = TCT - OP. VAV damper control loop disabled with output 0 during closed period. At end of each closed period, VAV damper commanded to last position from previous open period prior to reenabling control loop.
g. During TAV mode, cycles alternate until Vspt > Vm.
h. When first entering TAV mode, start with initial open period of duration RNDM*OP, where RNDM is random number between 0.0 and 1.0.

5.2.2.2. When in TAV mode, the active airflow setpoint, Vspt, shall be overridden to Vspt*.
5.2.2.3. For zones with CO2 sensors:
a. If CO2 concentration is less than 300 ppm, or the zone is in Unoccupied Mode for more than 2 hours and zone CO2 concentration exceeds 600 ppm, generate a Level 3 alarm. The alarm text shall identify the sensor and indicate that it may be out of calibration.
b. If CO2 concentration exceeds setpoint plus 10% for more than 10 minutes, generate a Level 3 alarm.
